description_long: This object is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. Its surface is painted with alternating diagonal red and white stripes, which are weathered and show signs of dirt and abrasion, indicating prolonged outdoor use. The barrier has a trapezoidal cross-section, with a broad, flat base for stability and a narrower top. The sides are slightly sloped, and the ends are flat, with subtle indents and surface irregularities visible. The paint is chipped and faded in places, revealing the rough, gray concrete beneath. There are no visible attachments, handles, or reflective strips, but the bold striping serves as a visual warning.
description: A weathered concrete jersey barrier with red and white diagonal stripes and visible surface wear, used for urban traffic control.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the narrow, flat face and sloped sides with visible wear and red paint along the edges.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the barrier with alternating red and white diagonal stripes and significant surface weathering.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view, similar to view 0, showing the flat end and sloped sides with less visible paint.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, with red and white stripes and visible scuffs and dirt on the surface.
